title: Luscious And Sorrowful.
content:
Beautiful, tender, wasting away for sorrow;
    Thus to-day; and how shall it be with thee to-morrow?
    Beautiful, tender - what else?
    A hope tells.

    Beautiful, tender, keeping the jubilee
    In the land of home together, past death and sea;
    No more change or death, no more
    Salt sea-shore.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Christina Georgina Rossetti, titled "Luscious And Sorrowful.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
